selection_reason: These two feature cards are the shortest route from v1.7 positioning to L3 proof: one proves measurement ownership, and the other connects product usage to social-impact evidence.

public_safety.public_export_rule: Publish only feature intent, public-safe fields, proof states, stop boundaries, and acceptance criteria. Keep account IDs, backend settings, raw screenshots, private reports, and identifiable records out of this repo.

feature_cards.why_critical: Kevin needs to show that GTM / GA4 / Ads and funnel work is not only planning, but production-visible proof that can guide product and growth decisions.
feature_cards.user_problem: 目前有 event plan and operating reports, but the proof state is scattered. A reviewer needs one place to see which events are planned, visible, blocked, missing, or no-op.

workflow.done_proof: Each event has a public-safe name and purpose.
Each event is marked planned, staging, production_visible, blocked, missing, or no_op.
Report-first update only; no external mutation.

acceptance_criteria: Every P0 event has a proof state.
Every proof state has a source-quality label.
Blocked or missing states include the next safe action.
No private account IDs or user-level data appear.
No external account change is made by the public artifact.
stop_conditions: The proof requires showing account identifiers.
The task would change GTM, GA4, Ads, production code, or backend settings.
A proof artifact contains user-level or sensitive answer data.
The dashboard starts treating missing data as zero.

feature_cards.feature_name: Impact metric tree and claim validation
feature_cards.why_critical: Kevin's market positioning depends on connecting social-impact goals, early adoption signals, product usage, and validated claims without overstating evidence.
feature_cards.user_problem: Application content has goals, adoption signals, and benefit claims, but public explanation needs to separate goal, early signal, verified proof, and needs-method-review.
not_allowed_inputs: Claims presented as verified without method review

workflow.done_proof: Each item is labeled goal, early_signal, verified, or needs_method_review.
Meaningful action is defined before impact is inferred.
Each claim has a method question and source-quality label.
The case says what is known, what is claimed, and what still needs validation.

acceptance_criteria: Every claim has a claim_status label.
Impact claims are not treated as verified until method review is complete.
Meaningful action is defined separately from visitor or registration counts.
No identifiable case or private survey content appears.
The output can be reused in a public-safe portfolio case.
stop_conditions: A claim is written as final proof without method review.
The task requires identifiable case details.
The metric would expose sensitive family, social-worker, or consultation data.
The output turns into individualized financial, legal, welfare, or HR advice.

next_version_candidates.candidate: Turn one feature card into a private implementation checklist after Kevin approval.
Add a public-safe portfolio case using the impact metric tree and proof dashboard.
